premise: I inherited my husband’s pristine $80,000 vintage Chris‑Craft and partnered with his sister to open a restoration shop, using the boat as collateral for a $150,000 loan; she forged a bill of sale, sold my boat for $95,000, and vanished, leaving me with the debt — I traced the vessel through federal records, proved forgery, and when she couldn’t pay the $220,000 judgment, I seized her lakefront cottage and now run sunset cruises from her former dock under a banner that reads ‘The Truth Floats.’

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My Sister-In-Law Sold My Boat, Forged The Bill, Left Me $150K In Debt. So I Watc
